# GitHub Issues Guide — FR-01: Account Registration

**Generated by:** github-issue-writer skill  
**Date:** 2026-06-16  
**Repository:** https://github.com/phatnguyen975/eshop-sut  
**Total issues:** 10  
**Source:** `qa-artifacts/bug-reports/FR01-bugs.md`

## Before You Start — One-Time Label Setup

> Skip this section if labels are already configured in the repository.

Go to: **https://github.com/phatnguyen975/eshop-sut → Settings → Labels → New label**

Create the following labels if they do not exist:

| Label                | Color     | Purpose                                 |
| -------------------- | --------- | --------------------------------------- |
| `bug`                | `#d73a4a` | All defect reports                      |
| `severity: fatal`    | `#b60205` | System crash / data loss                |
| `severity: serious`  | `#e4e669` | Core feature broken / security violated |
| `severity: medium`   | `#0075ca` | SRS deviation, feature still works      |
| `severity: cosmetic` | `#cfd3d7` | Minor UI issues only                    |
| `FR-01`              | `#5319e7` | Account Registration                    |
| `FR-07`              | `#1d76db` | Shopping Cart                           |
| `FR-17`              | `#0e8a16` | Coupon Management                       |
| `FR-03`              | `#fbca04` | Forgot Password                         |
| `security`           | `#b60205` | SEC-xx violations                       |
| `api`                | `#0052cc` | API-level bugs                          |
| `ui`                 | `#e4e669` | UI / frontend bugs                      |
| `dom`                | `#fbca04` | DOM / HTML structure bugs               |

## How to Use This Guide

1. Open: **https://github.com/phatnguyen975/eshop-sut/issues/new**
2. For each issue below:  
   a. Copy the **Title** exactly as shown into the Title field  
   b. Paste the **Body** content into the description area  
   c. Add the **Labels** listed (select from the dropdown)  
   d. If a screenshot is listed in the Evidence section: drag and drop the file into the body text area — GitHub will upload and embed it automatically  
   e. Click **Submit new issue**  
   f. Note the issue number from the URL (e.g., the URL becomes `.../issues/15` → number is `15`)
3. After posting ALL issues for this FR, return and type:
   ```
   Issue numbers for FR-01: BUG-001=#X, BUG-002=#Y, ...
   ```
   The agent will update `qa-artifacts/bug-reports/FR01-bugs.md` with these numbers.

## Issue 1 of 10

### BUG-001 — POST /api/register inserts user password as plaintext

**Title:**

```
[BUG][FR01] POST /api/register inserts user password as plaintext
```

**Labels:** `bug`, `severity: serious`, `FR-01`, `api`, `security`

**Screenshot to attach:** None — API test, no screenshot required

**Body:**

````markdown
## Summary

POST /api/register inserts user password as plaintext instead of bcrypt hash when storing in DB (per SEC-01)

## Bug Details

| Field          | Value                                                                                                                                                                |
| -------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **Bug ID**     | BUG-001                                                                                                                                                              |
| **Linked TCs** | FR01-EP-001, FR01-EP-020, FR01-BVA-003, FR01-BVA-004, FR01-BVA-005, FR01-BVA-008, FR01-BVA-009, FR01-BVA-010, FR01-BVA-011, FR01-BVA-012, FR01-BVA-015, FR01-BVA-016 |
| **Channel**    | API + State                                                                                                                                                          |
| **Severity**   | Serious                                                                                                                                                              |
| **Priority**   | High                                                                                                                                                                 |

## Environment

| Component    | Value                 |
| ------------ | --------------------- |
| OS / Device  | Ubuntu (WSL)          |
| Runtime Env  | N/A (cURL test)       |
| Backend      | http://localhost:3000 |
| Frontend/App | N/A (API-only test)   |
| Test Account | ep001@test.com        |

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Ensure the EShop backend is running at http://localhost:3000.
2. Send the following request:
   ```bash
   curl -s -X POST http://localhost:3000/api/register \
      -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
      -d '{"name":"Nguyen Van A","email":"ep001@test.com","password":"Test@123"}'
   ```
3. Observe the HTTP response status code.
4. Observe the response body.
5. Query the database:
   ```bash
   sqlite3 backend/database.sqlite "SELECT password FROM users WHERE email='ep001@test.com';"
   ```

## Expected Behavior

Per **SEC-01**:

> Passwords must not be stored as plaintext.

Specifically:

- HTTP 200 OK
- Response body: `{"message": "User registered successfully", "id": <positive integer>}`
- DB state: Password stored as bcrypt hash (starts with `$2`).

## Actual Behavior

- HTTP Status: `HTTP 200`
- Response Body:
   ```json
   { "message": "User registered successfully", "id": 1 }
   ```
- DB State: Password is stored as plaintext `Test@123` instead of a bcrypt hash.

## Severity Rationale

**Serious:** SEC-01 is explicitly violated, storing user passwords in plaintext which poses a massive security vulnerability.

## Priority Rationale

**High:** Security vulnerability that exposes user credentials, blocking secure deployment.

## Evidence

None — API test, no screenshot required.

> Reported by: 23127449 - Nguyễn Tấn Phát | Date: 2026-06-14
````

## Issue 2 of 10

### BUG-002 — POST /api/register returns HTTP 200 on invalid input

**Title:**

```
[BUG][FR01] POST /api/register returns HTTP 200 on invalid input
```

**Labels:** `bug`, `severity: serious`, `FR-01`, `api`

**Screenshot to attach:** None — API test, no screenshot required

**Body:**

````markdown
## Summary

POST /api/register returns HTTP 200 instead of HTTP 400 when missing/invalid fields are submitted (per FR-01)

## Bug Details

| Field          | Value                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             |
| -------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **Bug ID**     | BUG-002                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           |
| **Linked TCs** | FR01-EP-002, FR01-EP-003, FR01-EP-004, FR01-EP-005, FR01-EP-006, FR01-EP-008, FR01-EP-009, FR01-EP-010, FR01-EP-011, FR01-EP-012, FR01-EP-013, FR01-EP-014, FR01-EP-015, FR01-EP-016, FR01-EP-017, FR01-BVA-001, FR01-BVA-002, FR01-BVA-006, FR01-BVA-007, FR01-BVA-013, FR01-BVA-014, FR01-BVA-017, FR01-BVA-018 |
| **Channel**    | API                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |
| **Severity**   | Serious                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           |
| **Priority**   | High                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              |

## Environment

| Component    | Value                 |
| ------------ | --------------------- |
| OS / Device  | Ubuntu (WSL)          |
| Runtime Env  | N/A (cURL test)       |
| Backend      | http://localhost:3000 |
| Frontend/App | N/A (API-only test)   |
| Test Account | ep002@test.com        |

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Ensure the EShop backend is running at http://localhost:3000.
2. Send the following request:
   ```bash
   curl -s -X POST http://localhost:3000/api/register \
      -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
      -d '{"name":"","email":"ep002@test.com","password":"Test@123"}'
   ```
3. Observe the HTTP response status code.
4. Observe the response body.

## Expected Behavior

Per **FR-01**:

> The system must validate input fields (Name is required, Email must be valid format, Password strength requirements, etc).

Specifically:

- HTTP 400 Bad Request
- Response body: Error message indicating Name is required.
- DB state: No new user created in DB.

## Actual Behavior

- HTTP Status: `HTTP 200`
- Response Body:
   ```json
   { "message": "User registered successfully", "id": 4 }
   ```

## Severity Rationale

**Serious:** Complete absence of backend input validation on the registration endpoint allows garbage data and empty required fields to be saved.

## Priority Rationale

**High:** Data integrity risk affecting core user registration flow.

## Evidence

None — API test, no screenshot required.

> Reported by: 23127449 - Nguyễn Tấn Phát | Date: 2026-06-14
````

## Issue 3 of 10

### BUG-003 — POST /api/register allows duplicate emails

**Title:**

```
[BUG][FR01] POST /api/register allows duplicate emails
```

**Labels:** `bug`, `severity: serious`, `FR-01`, `api`

**Screenshot to attach:** None — API test, no screenshot required

**Body:**

````markdown
## Summary

POST /api/register returns HTTP 200 instead of HTTP 409 when email already exists in DB (per FR-01)

## Bug Details

| Field          | Value       |
| -------------- | ----------- |
| **Bug ID**     | BUG-003     |
| **Linked TCs** | FR01-EP-007 |
| **Channel**    | API         |
| **Severity**   | Serious     |
| **Priority**   | High        |

## Environment

| Component    | Value                 |
| ------------ | --------------------- |
| OS / Device  | Ubuntu (WSL)          |
| Runtime Env  | N/A (cURL test)       |
| Backend      | http://localhost:3000 |
| Frontend/App | N/A (API-only test)   |
| Test Account | existing@test.com     |

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Ensure the EShop backend is running at http://localhost:3000.
2. Send the following request with an already registered email:
   ```bash
   curl -s -X POST http://localhost:3000/api/register \
      -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
      -d '{"name":"Duplicate User","email":"test@eshop.com","password":"Test@123"}'
   ```
3. Observe the HTTP response status code.
4. Observe the response body.

## Expected Behavior

Per **FR-01**:

> System must reject registration if email already exists in the system.

Specifically:

- HTTP 409 Conflict
- Response body: `{"message": "Email already exists"}`
- DB state: No additional user record created.

## Actual Behavior

- HTTP Status: `HTTP 200`
- Response Body:
   ```json
   { "message": "User registered successfully", "id": 5 }
   ```

## Severity Rationale

**Serious:** Allows multiple users to register with the exact same email, violating core business rules and breaking login functionality.

## Priority Rationale

**High:** Causes critical data integrity issues for the authentication flow.

## Evidence

None — API test, no screenshot required.

> Reported by: 23127449 - Nguyễn Tấn Phát | Date: 2026-06-14
````

## Issue 4 of 10

### BUG-004 — UI password validation rejects valid special chars

**Title:**

```
[BUG][FR01] UI password validation rejects valid special chars
```

**Labels:** `bug`, `severity: medium`, `FR-01`, `ui`

**Screenshot to attach:** `evidence/screenshots/FR01/BUG-004.png` — drag into body before submitting

**Body:**

```markdown
## Summary

Registration form rejects valid standard special characters in password instead of accepting them when submitting (per FR-01)

## Bug Details

| Field          | Value                     |
| -------------- | ------------------------- |
| **Bug ID**     | BUG-004                   |
| **Linked TCs** | FR01-EP-001, FR01-BVA-003 |
| **Channel**    | UI                        |
| **Severity**   | Medium                    |
| **Priority**   | High                      |

## Environment

| Component    | Value                 |
| ------------ | --------------------- |
| OS / Device  | Ubuntu (WSL)          |
| Runtime Env  | Edge (Web Browser)    |
| Backend      | http://localhost:3000 |
| Frontend/App | http://localhost:5173 |
| Test Account | ep001@test.com        |

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Navigate to: http://localhost:5173/register
2. Fill `Name` with `Nguyen Van A`
3. Fill `Email` with `ep001@test.com`
4. Fill `Password` with `Test@123`
5. Click the "Register" button.
6. Observe: UI validation error message for the password field.

## Expected Behavior

Per **FR-01**:

> Password must contain at least one special character from the allowed set.

Specifically:

- Form is submitted successfully without validation errors for standard special characters like `@`.

## Actual Behavior

- UI: Password incorrectly rejected due to standard special character (requires space instead).

## Severity Rationale

**Medium:** Incorrect UI validation blocks valid passwords, deviating from SRS.

## Priority Rationale

**High:** Blocks users from registering with common, standard strong passwords.

## Evidence

Drag and drop the screenshot here — GitHub will auto-upload it.

_Screenshot file: `evidence/screenshots/FR01/BUG-004.png`_

> Reported by: 23127449 - Nguyễn Tấn Phát | Date: 2026-06-14
```

## Summary

Registration form uses native HTML5 browser tooltip instead of custom inline error message when required fields are empty (per FR-22)

## Bug Details

| Field          | Value                                 |
| -------------- | ------------------------------------- |
| **Bug ID**     | BUG-005                               |
| **Linked TCs** | FR01-EP-002, FR01-EP-008, FR01-EP-016 |
| **Channel**    | UI                                    |
| **Severity**   | Cosmetic                              |
| **Priority**   | Low                                   |

## Environment

| Component    | Value                 |
| ------------ | --------------------- |
| OS / Device  | Ubuntu (WSL)          |
| Runtime Env  | Edge (Web Browser)    |
| Backend      | http://localhost:3000 |
| Frontend/App | http://localhost:5173 |
| Test Account | ep002@test.com        |

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Navigate to: http://localhost:5173/register
2. Fill `Name` with `""` (leave empty)
3. Fill `Email` with `ep002@test.com`
4. Fill `Password` with `Test@123`
5. Click the "Register" button.
6. Observe: Error presentation format.

## Expected Behavior

Per **FR-22**:

> Forms must display custom inline error messages for missing required fields.

Specifically:

- Custom inline error message displayed for the empty Name field.

## Actual Behavior

- UI: Relies on native browser HTML5 tooltip instead of custom inline error message.

## Severity Rationale

**Cosmetic:** Does not prevent registration but deviates from the expected custom UI design language.

## Priority Rationale

**Low:** Purely a visual / UX inconsistency.

## Issue 8 of 10

### BUG-008 — Page lacks required `<h1>` tag

**Title:**

```
[BUG][FR01] Page lacks required <h1> tag
```

**Labels:** `bug`, `severity: cosmetic`, `FR-01`, `dom`

**Screenshot to attach:** `evidence/screenshots/FR01/BUG-008.png` — drag into body before submitting

**Body:**

````markdown
## Summary

Registration page has 0 `<h1>` tags instead of exactly 1 (per FR-21)

## Bug Details

| Field          | Value       |
| -------------- | ----------- |
| **Bug ID**     | BUG-008     |
| **Linked TCs** | FR01-DOM-H1 |
| **Channel**    | DOM         |
| **Severity**   | Cosmetic    |
| **Priority**   | Low         |

## Environment

| Component    | Value                 |
| ------------ | --------------------- |
| OS / Device  | Ubuntu (WSL)          |
| Runtime Env  | Edge (Web Browser)    |
| Backend      | http://localhost:3000 |
| Frontend/App | http://localhost:5173 |
| Test Account | N/A                   |

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Navigate to: http://localhost:5173/register
2. Open DevTools: F12 → Console tab.
3. Paste the following and press Enter:

   ```js
   (function FR01DOMChecks() {
     "use strict";

     let pass = 0,
       fail = 0;
     const results = [];

     function check(tcId, description, expected, actualFn) {
       let actual, status;
       try {
         actual = actualFn();
         const ok =
           expected === "EXISTS"
             ? actual !== null && actual !== undefined && actual !== "NOT_FOUND"
             : String(actual) === String(expected);
         status = ok ? "PASS" : "FAIL";
       } catch (e) {
         actual = "ERROR: " + e.message;
         status = "FAIL";
       }
       status === "PASS" ? pass++ : fail++;
       results.push({ tcId, description, expected, actual, status });
     }

     // DOM-H1: Exactly 1 <h1> tag (per FR-21 — all web pages)
     check("FR01-DOM-H1", "Page has exactly 1 <h1> tag (per FR-21)", "1", () =>
       String(document.querySelectorAll("h1").length),
     );

     console.log(
       "%c━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━",
       "font-weight: bold",
     );
     console.log(
       "%c FR-01 DOM CHECK RESULTS",
       "font-weight: bold; font-size: 14px",
     );
     console.log(
       "%c━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━",
       "font-weight: bold",
     );

     results.forEach((r) => {
       const color =
         r.status === "PASS"
           ? "color: green; font-weight: bold"
           : "color: red; font-weight: bold";
       console.log(
         `%c[${r.status}] %c${r.tcId} — ${r.description}`,
         color,
         "color: inherit",
       );
       console.log(`       Expected: ${r.expected} | Actual: ${r.actual}`);
     });

     console.log(
       "%c━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━",
       "font-weight: bold",
     );
     console.log(
       `TOTAL: %c${pass} PASS%c | %c${fail} FAIL`,
       "color: green; font-weight: bold",
       "color: inherit",
       "color: red; font-weight: bold",
     );
   })();
   ```

4. Observe the output in the console.

## Expected Behavior

Per **FR-21**:

> Every web page must have exactly one `<h1>` tag.

Specifically:

- DOM should contain 1 `<h1>` element.

## Actual Behavior

- UI: DOM has 0 `<h1>` elements.

## Severity Rationale

**Cosmetic:** DOM structure deviation that doesn't block functional workflows.

## Priority Rationale

**Low:** Minor SEO / Accessibility deviation.

## Evidence

Drag and drop the screenshot here — GitHub will auto-upload it.

_Screenshot file: `evidence/screenshots/FR01/BUG-008.png`_

> Reported by: 23127449 - Nguyễn Tấn Phát | Date: 2026-06-14
````

## Issue 9 of 10

### BUG-009 — Email input lacks `type="email"`

**Title:**

```
[BUG][FR01] Email input lacks type="email"
```

**Labels:** `bug`, `severity: cosmetic`, `FR-01`, `dom`

**Screenshot to attach:** `evidence/screenshots/FR01/BUG-009.png` — drag into body before submitting

**Body:**

````markdown
## Summary

Registration page email input lacks `type="email"` attribute instead of including it (per FR-22)

## Bug Details

| Field          | Value          |
| -------------- | -------------- |
| **Bug ID**     | BUG-009        |
| **Linked TCs** | FR01-DOM-EMAIL |
| **Channel**    | DOM            |
| **Severity**   | Cosmetic       |
| **Priority**   | Low            |

## Environment

| Component    | Value                 |
| ------------ | --------------------- |
| OS / Device  | Ubuntu (WSL)          |
| Runtime Env  | Edge (Web Browser)    |
| Backend      | http://localhost:3000 |
| Frontend/App | http://localhost:5173 |
| Test Account | N/A                   |

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Navigate to: http://localhost:5173/register
2. Open DevTools: F12 → Console tab.
3. Paste the following and press Enter:

   ```js
   (function FR01DOMChecks() {
     "use strict";

     let pass = 0,
       fail = 0;
     const results = [];

     function check(tcId, description, expected, actualFn) {
       let actual, status;
       try {
         actual = actualFn();
         const ok =
           expected === "EXISTS"
             ? actual !== null && actual !== undefined && actual !== "NOT_FOUND"
             : String(actual) === String(expected);
         status = ok ? "PASS" : "FAIL";
       } catch (e) {
         actual = "ERROR: " + e.message;
         status = "FAIL";
       }
       status === "PASS" ? pass++ : fail++;
       results.push({ tcId, description, expected, actual, status });
     }

     // DOM-EMAIL: Email input type="email" (per FR-22)
     check(
       "FR01-DOM-EMAIL",
       'Email input has type="email" (per FR-22)',
       "email",
       () => {
         const el =
           document.querySelector(
             'input[name="email"], input[autocomplete="email"]',
           ) || document.querySelector('input[type="email"]');
         return el
           ? el.type
           : "No email field found or input type is not email";
       },
     );

     console.log(
       "%c━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━",
       "font-weight: bold",
     );
     console.log(
       "%c FR-01 DOM CHECK RESULTS",
       "font-weight: bold; font-size: 14px",
     );
     console.log(
       "%c━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━",
       "font-weight: bold",
     );

     results.forEach((r) => {
       const color =
         r.status === "PASS"
           ? "color: green; font-weight: bold"
           : "color: red; font-weight: bold";
       console.log(
         `%c[${r.status}] %c${r.tcId} — ${r.description}`,
         color,
         "color: inherit",
       );
       console.log(`       Expected: ${r.expected} | Actual: ${r.actual}`);
     });

     console.log(
       "%c━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━",
       "font-weight: bold",
     );
     console.log(
       `TOTAL: %c${pass} PASS%c | %c${fail} FAIL`,
       "color: green; font-weight: bold",
       "color: inherit",
       "color: red; font-weight: bold",
     );
   })();
   ```

4. Observe the missing type attribute.

## Expected Behavior

Per **FR-22**:

> Email inputs must use `type="email"`.

Specifically:

- DOM should return "email" for the input type.

## Actual Behavior

- UI: Email input has type text or lacks type="email".

## Severity Rationale

**Cosmetic:** Deviates from standard HTML5 semantics.

## Priority Rationale

**Low:** Mobile keyboard optimizations won't trigger for this field.

## Evidence

Drag and drop the screenshot here — GitHub will auto-upload it.

_Screenshot file: `evidence/screenshots/FR01/BUG-009.png`_

> Reported by: 23127449 - Nguyễn Tấn Phát | Date: 2026-06-14
````

## Issue 10 of 10

### BUG-010 — Required fields lack `*` marker in labels

**Title:**

```
[BUG][FR01] Required fields lack * marker in labels
```

**Labels:** `bug`, `severity: cosmetic`, `FR-01`, `dom`

**Screenshot to attach:** `evidence/screenshots/FR01/BUG-010.png` — drag into body before submitting

**Body:**

````markdown
## Summary

Registration page required fields lack `*` marker in labels instead of including them (per FR-22)

## Bug Details

| Field          | Value         |
| -------------- | ------------- |
| **Bug ID**     | BUG-010       |
| **Linked TCs** | FR01-DOM-STAR |
| **Channel**    | DOM           |
| **Severity**   | Cosmetic      |
| **Priority**   | Low           |

## Environment

| Component    | Value                 |
| ------------ | --------------------- |
| OS / Device  | Ubuntu (WSL)          |
| Runtime Env  | Edge (Web Browser)    |
| Backend      | http://localhost:3000 |
| Frontend/App | http://localhost:5173 |
| Test Account | N/A                   |

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Navigate to: http://localhost:5173/register
2. Open DevTools: F12 → Console tab.
3. Paste the following and press Enter:

   ```js
   (function FR01DOMChecks() {
     "use strict";

     let pass = 0,
       fail = 0;
     const results = [];

     function check(tcId, description, expected, actualFn) {
       let actual, status;
       try {
         actual = actualFn();
         const ok =
           expected === "EXISTS"
             ? actual !== null && actual !== undefined && actual !== "NOT_FOUND"
             : String(actual) === String(expected);
         status = ok ? "PASS" : "FAIL";
       } catch (e) {
         actual = "ERROR: " + e.message;
         status = "FAIL";
       }
       status === "PASS" ? pass++ : fail++;
       results.push({ tcId, description, expected, actual, status });
     }

     // DOM-STAR: Required fields have * label marker (per FR-22)
     check(
       "FR01-DOM-STAR",
       "Required fields have * in label (per FR-22)",
       "Has * markers",
       () => {
         const labels = Array.from(document.querySelectorAll("label"));
         const stars = labels.filter((l) => l.textContent.trim().includes("*"));
         if (stars.length > 0) return "Has * markers";
         return "0 labels with *";
       },
     );

     console.log(
       "%c━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━",
       "font-weight: bold",
     );
     console.log(
       "%c FR-01 DOM CHECK RESULTS",
       "font-weight: bold; font-size: 14px",
     );
     console.log(
       "%c━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━",
       "font-weight: bold",
     );

     results.forEach((r) => {
       const color =
         r.status === "PASS"
           ? "color: green; font-weight: bold"
           : "color: red; font-weight: bold";
       console.log(
         `%c[${r.status}] %c${r.tcId} — ${r.description}`,
         color,
         "color: inherit",
       );
       console.log(`       Expected: ${r.expected} | Actual: ${r.actual}`);
     });

     console.log(
       "%c━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━",
       "font-weight: bold",
     );
     console.log(
       `TOTAL: %c${pass} PASS%c | %c${fail} FAIL`,
       "color: green; font-weight: bold",
       "color: inherit",
       "color: red; font-weight: bold",
     );
   })();
   ```

4. Observe the FAIL output in the console.

## Expected Behavior

Per **FR-22**:

> Required fields must be marked with a `*` in their labels.

Specifically:

- UI labels should visually show `*`.

## Actual Behavior

- UI: 0 labels found with `*`.

## Severity Rationale

**Cosmetic:** Visual UX deviation from SRS.

## Priority Rationale

**Low:** Minor UX inconsistency.

## Evidence

Drag and drop the screenshot here — GitHub will auto-upload it.

_Screenshot file: `evidence/screenshots/FR01/BUG-010.png`_

> Reported by: 23127449 - Nguyễn Tấn Phát | Date: 2026-06-14
````
